results.description: DESCRIPTION:THE GOAL OF THIS PROJECT IS TO DEVELOP A FRAMEWORK FOR EVALUATING CHANGES IN NEIGHBORHOOD AIR POLLUTION DISTRIBUTIONS ARISING FROM ALTERNATIVE FUTURE SCENARIOS FOR PUBLIC TRANSPORTATION AND ELECTRICITY GENERATION, UTILIZING AN ATMOSPHERIC POLLUTION DISPERSION MODELING TO ESTIMATE PUBLIC TRANSPORTATION AND ELECTRICITY GENERATION IMPACTS ON AIR POLLUTION CONCENTRATIONS.ACTIVITIES:SPECIFIC OBJECTIVES ARE TO: 1) QUANTIFY THE EXISTING DISPARITIES IN AIR POLLUTION EXPOSURE TO PUBLIC TRANSPORTATION AND ELECTRICITY GENERATION IN MARICOPA COUNTY; 2) COLLECT DATA ON PLANNED PUBLIC TRANSIT INTERVENTIONS FOR DECARBONIZATION OF THE TRANSPORTATION FLEET IN MARICOPA COUNTY; 3) EVALUATE AIR QUALITY AND ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE IMPACTS OF FUTURE PUBLIC TRANSIT SCENARIOS; AND 4) DEVELOP A VISUALIZATION TOOL OF PROPOSED PLANNED INFRASTRUCTURE FOR STAKEHOLDERS AND COMMUNITIES.SUBRECIPIENT:$314,618 IS BUDGETED TO UNLIMITED POTENTIAL. THEY WILL PROVIDE INPUT TO DEVELOP A FRAMEWORK FOR ANALYZING PROPOSED TRANSPORTATION INFRASTRUCTURE PROJECTS, CREATE COMMUNITY FOCUS GROUPS TO UNDERSTAND THE LOCAL PERCEPTIONS OF AIR QUALITY AND PREFERENCES OVER CERTAIN PROGRAMS TO REDUCE AIR POLLUTION, AND COORDINATE COMMUNITY OUTREACH ACTIVITIES TO DISSEMINATE THE RESULTS. OUTCOMES:DELIVERABLES INCLUDE ANNUAL AND FINAL REPORTS. OTHER EXPECTED DELIVERABLES INCLUDE A DATABASE OF POLLUTION CONCENTRATIONS DUE TO EXISTING ELECTRICITY GENERATION AND PUBLIC TRANSIT INFRASTRUCTURE IN UNDERSERVED COMMUNITIES, AN INVENTORY OF PLANNED PUBLIC TRANSIT DEVELOPMENTS AND COMMUNITY SURVEY ABOUT POTENTIAL PROGRAMS AND INTERVENTIONS WITH ASSOCIATED EMISSIONS IN MARICOPA COUNTY, AND A DATABASE OF POLLUTION EXPOSURE AND ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE STATUS AT THE CENSUS BLOCK GROUP LEVEL. EXPECTED OUTCOMES INCLUDE INCREASED KNOWLEDGE THAT THE PROJECT'S STAKEHOLDERS CAN INCORPORATE IN THEIR FUTURE ENERGY TRANSITION DECISIONS AND HELP CITIES IDENTIFY WHICH PLANNED ELECTRIFICATION AND TRANSPORTATION POLICIES CAN REDUCE ENVIRONMENTAL DISPARITIES IN UNDERSERVED COMMUNITIES. DIRECT BENEFICIARIES OF THIS PROJECT INCLUDE THE RESIDENTS OF MARICOPA COUNTY IN ARIZONA, HUMAN HEALTH AND THE ENVIRONMENT AND THE GENERAL PUBLIC.
